summarization.a: The figure shows a spectral intensity plot with two curves representing H2 plasma and an H2 plasma in an ALD cycle.
summarization.b: The figure displays a line chart comparing the intensity at 388 nm of an H2 plasma and an H2 plasma in an ALD cycle over time.

data_extraction.a:
| Wavelength (nm) | Intensity (a.u.) H2 plasma|Intensity (a.u.) H2 plasma after ALD cycle|
|---|---|---|
| 388 | peak | peak |
| 500 | peaks |peaks |
| 600 | peaks |peaks |
| 660 | H peak | H peak |

a.question: Are a lot of the ligand species incorporated in the hydrogen plasma?
a.answer: a	It seems that there is major overlap between all the species present between the two plasmas, so not a lot of different species are incorporated.

a.question: At what wavelength is the highest intensity measured?
a.answer: a	The highest intensity is measured at 660 nm.

b.question: What is the saturation time for the H2 plasma in the deposition process of TiN?
b.answer: b	The saturation time is 4 s.

b.question: Will the intensity of the peak go to zero for the ALD cycle data, and if not, why not?
b.answer: b	No the intensity will not go to zero if the plasma is running  there will always be the background of the peak in the H2 plasma itself.
